Implement the following plan:

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

Pattern from the repo:
- `config/` = XDG config file management (e.g. `config/jj/`, `config/ghostty/`)
- `home-manager/programs/` = package + program option configuration (e.g. `home-manager/programs/tms/`)

## Implementation

### 1. Install package in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];` alongside the existing `programs.tmux`:

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = {
    enable = true;
    # ... unchanged
  };
}
```

### 2. Create `config/tmuxinator/` (new config directory)

**`config/tmuxinator/default.nix`** — following `config/jj/default.nix` pattern:
```nix
{ ... }:
{
  xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator;
}
```

Register in **`config/default.nix`** (alongside other entries):
```nix
./tmuxinator
```

### 3. Create YAML layout files in `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/`

(Mirrors `~/.config/tmuxinator/` under XDG)

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 4. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (tmuxinator handles attach/switch-client automatically by detecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 5. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

Default sessions use tmuxinator on first create; ad-hoc sessions use plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to Modify/Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ]` |
| `config/default.nix` | Add `./tmuxinator` entry |
| `config/tmuxinator/default.nix` | **create** — `xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator` |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/primary.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/mobile.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/desktop.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/work.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tpo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tdo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator for default sessions |

## Verification
1. After `home-manager switch`, run `tpo` with no existing `primary` session → tmuxinator creates it with 3 windows (editor, shell, server)
2. Run `tpo` again → attaches/switches normally without re-applying layout
3. Run `tss`, select `mobile` (non-existent) → tmuxinator creates it with 2 windows
4. Run `tss`, select an existing session → switch/attach as before
5. Run `tss`, type a new ad-hoc name → plain `tmux new-session` (no YAML needed)

function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end

Both files updated. Here's the logic:

**When `work` session doesn't exist** (both in `two` and `tss`):
1. Extract the resurrect restore script path from tmux's live key bindings — e.g. `/nix/store/.../resurrect/scripts/restore.sh` — using `string match -rg` on `tmux list-keys` output. This avoids hardcoding any nix store hash.
2. Run `tmux run-shell "$restore"` to restore the last saved state (resurrect restores all previously saved sessions).
3. Check if `work` came back — if yes, attach/switch to it.
4. If `work` wasn't in the saved state (or resurrect isn't available), fall back to `tmuxinator start work`.

**When `work` already exists** — behaves the same as before (attach or switch-client).

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

Pattern from the repo:
- `config/` = XDG config file management (e.g. `config/jj/`, `config/ghostty/`)
- `home-manager/programs/` = package + program option configuration (e.g. `home-manager/programs/tms/`)

## Implementation

### 1. Install package in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];` alongside the existing `programs.tmux`:

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = {
    enable = true;
    # ... unchanged
  };
}
```

### 2. Create `config/tmuxinator/` (new config directory)

**`config/tmuxinator/default.nix`** — following `config/jj/default.nix` pattern:
```nix
{ ... }:
{
  xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator;
}
```

Register in **`config/default.nix`** (alongside other entries):
```nix
./tmuxinator
```

### 3. Create YAML layout files in `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/`

(Mirrors `~/.config/tmuxinator/` under XDG)

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 4. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (tmuxinator handles attach/switch-client automatically by detecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 5. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

Default sessions use tmuxinator on first create; ad-hoc sessions use plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to Modify/Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ]` |
| `config/default.nix` | Add `./tmuxinator` entry |
| `config/tmuxinator/default.nix` | **create** — `xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator` |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/primary.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/mobile.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/desktop.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/work.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tpo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tdo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator for default sessions |

## Verification
1. After `home-manager switch`, run `tpo` with no existing `primary` session → tmuxinator creates it with 3 windows (editor, shell, server)
2. Run `tpo` again → attaches/switches normally without re-applying layout
3. Run `tss`, select `mobile` (non-existent) → tmuxinator creates it with 2 windows
4. Run `tss`, select an existing session → switch/attach as before
5. Run `tss`, type a new ad-hoc name → plain `tmux new-session` (no YAML needed)

function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  # Build candidate list: default sessions first, then any extra existing sessions
  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if test "$selected" = work
      set -l restore (tmux list-keys 2>/dev/null | string match -rg '(/\S+/resurrect/scripts/restore\.sh)')
      set -l restore $restore[1]
      if test -n "$restore"
        tmux run-shell "$restore"
      end
      if tmux has-session -t work 2>/dev/null
        if test -n "$TMUX"
          tmux switch-client -t work
        else
          tmux attach-session -t work
        end
      else
        tmuxinator start work
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end

# Plan: Tmux Session/Window Search Improvements

## Context
The setup already has `_tss_function` (session picker, `prefix+S` / `tss`) and `tmux-sessionx` (`prefix+o`).
The gaps are:
1. `_tss_function` is blind — no preview of what windows are in a session, and no way to kill a session from the picker.
2. There is no cross-session **window** fuzzy picker — no way to jump directly to a named window by name across all sessions.
3. No quick last-session toggle keybinding in tmux.

## Improvements

### 1. Enhance `_tss_function` — preview + kill binding

Add two fzf flags:
- `--preview 'tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null'` — shows windows of hovered session
- `--bind 'ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort'` — kills the selected session and exits

Only the `fzf` call on line 13 changes; all session logic stays the same.

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish`

```fish
  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')
```

---

### 2. New `_twf_function` — cross-session window fuzzy picker

Lists every window across every session as `session:index  window_name`. Selecting one switches to that session then that window.

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish`

```fish
function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_twf_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetical between `_tss_function` and `_two_function`)
- Add `twf = "_twf_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

### 3. `tmux.conf` — last-session toggle + window picker bind

Add two lines to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`** in the bindings section:

```
# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _twf_function'"
```

`Tab` is currently unbound. `W` is currently unbound (lowercase `w` is default select-window by index, not bound here).

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Add preview + ctrl-x kill to fzf call |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish` | **create** — cross-session window picker |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_twf_function` to list + `twf` abbr |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `bind Tab switch-client -l` and `bind W` |

## Verification

1. `tss` → hover over a session → right panel shows numbered windows
2. `tss` → hover over a session → press `ctrl-x` → session is killed, picker closes
3. `twf` (or `prefix+W` inside tmux) → lists all windows from all sessions → select one → lands in the right session+window
4. Inside tmux, press `prefix+Tab` → switches to the previously active session instantly

# Plan: Tmux Session Presets via tmuxinator

## Context
The 4 named sessions (`primary`, `mobile`, `desktop`, `work`) are created bare with `tmux new-session -A -s <name>`. The goal is to have each session automatically start with predefined windows when first created (editor, shell, server, etc.). **tmuxinator** is the chosen mechanism — it reads a YAML layout file per session and wires up windows/panes on first create, then falls back to normal attach on subsequent invocations.

Pattern from the repo:
- `config/` = XDG config file management (e.g. `config/jj/`, `config/ghostty/`)
- `home-manager/programs/` = package + program option configuration (e.g. `home-manager/programs/tms/`)

## Implementation

### 1. Install package in `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix`

Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];` alongside the existing `programs.tmux`:

```nix
{ pkgs, ... }:
{
  home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ];

  programs.tmux = {
    enable = true;
    # ... unchanged
  };
}
```

### 2. Create `config/tmuxinator/` (new config directory)

**`config/tmuxinator/default.nix`** — following `config/jj/default.nix` pattern:
```nix
{ ... }:
{
  xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator;
}
```

Register in **`config/default.nix`** (alongside other entries):
```nix
./tmuxinator
```

### 3. Create YAML layout files in `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/`

(Mirrors `~/.config/tmuxinator/` under XDG)

**`primary.yml`** — editor + shell + server:
```yaml
name: primary
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`mobile.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: mobile
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`desktop.yml`** — editor + dev server:
```yaml
name: desktop
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - server: bun run dev
```

**`work.yml`** — editor + shell:
```yaml
name: work
windows:
  - editor: nvim
  - shell:
```

### 4. Update the 4 individual fish functions

Pattern: if session exists → attach/switch normally; if new → `tmuxinator start <name>` (tmuxinator handles attach/switch-client automatically by detecting `$TMUX`).

**`_tpo_function.fish`**:
```fish
function _tpo_function --description "Attach to tmux primary session"
  if tmux has-session -t primary 2>/dev/null
    if test -n "$TMUX"
      tmux switch-client -t primary
    else
      tmux attach-session -t primary
    end
  else
    tmuxinator start primary
  end
end
```

Same pattern for `_tmo_function` (`mobile`), `_tdo_function` (`desktop`), `_two_function` (`work`).

### 5. Update `_tss_function.fish` (sessionizer)

Default sessions use tmuxinator on first create; ad-hoc sessions use plain `tmux new-session`:

```fish
function _tss_function --description "Fuzzy-pick or create a tmux session"
  set -l default_sessions primary mobile desktop work

  set -l existing (tmux list-sessions -F '#S' 2>/dev/null)
  set -l candidates $default_sessions
  for s in $existing
    if not contains $s $default_sessions
      set -a candidates $s
    end
  end

  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf --prompt="session> " --height=40%)

  if test -n "$selected"
    if tmux has-session -t "$selected" 2>/dev/null
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    else if contains $selected $default_sessions
      tmuxinator start "$selected"
    else
      tmux new-session -d -s "$selected"
      if test -n "$TMUX"
        tmux switch-client -t "$selected"
      else
        tmux attach-session -t "$selected"
      end
    end
  end
end
```

## Files to Modify/Create
| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/default.nix` | Add `home.packages = [ pkgs.tmuxinator ]` |
| `config/default.nix` | Add `./tmuxinator` entry |
| `config/tmuxinator/default.nix` | **create** — `xdg.configFile."tmuxinator".source = ./tmuxinator` |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/primary.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/mobile.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/desktop.yml` | **create** |
| `config/tmuxinator/tmuxinator/work.yml` | **create** |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tpo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tmo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tdo_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_two_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator on first create |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Use tmuxinator for default sessions |

## Verification
1. After `home-manager switch`, run `tpo` with no existing `primary` session → tmuxinator creates it with 3 windows (editor, shell, server)
2. Run `tpo` again → attaches/switches normally without re-applying layout
3. Run `tss`, select `mobile` (non-existent) → tmuxinator creates it with 2 windows
4. Run `tss`, select an existing session → switch/attach as before
5. Run `tss`, type a new ad-hoc name → plain `tmux new-session` (no YAML needed)

# Plan: Tmux Session/Window Search Improvements

## Context
The setup already has `_tss_function` (session picker, `prefix+S` / `tss`) and `tmux-sessionx` (`prefix+o`).
The gaps are:
1. `_tss_function` is blind — no preview of what windows are in a session, and no way to kill a session from the picker.
2. There is no cross-session **window** fuzzy picker — no way to jump directly to a named window by name across all sessions.
3. No quick last-session toggle keybinding in tmux.

## Improvements

### 1. Enhance `_tss_function` — preview + kill binding

Add two fzf flags:
- `--preview 'tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null'` — shows windows of hovered session
- `--bind 'ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort'` — kills the selected session and exits

Only the `fzf` call on line 13 changes; all session logic stays the same.

**File:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish`

```fish
  set -l selected (printf '%s\n' $candidates | fzf \
    --prompt="session> " \
    --height=40% \
    --preview='tmux list-windows -F "#I: #W" -t {} 2>/dev/null' \
    --bind='ctrl-x:execute-silent(tmux kill-session -t {})+abort')
```

---

### 2. New `_twf_function` — cross-session window fuzzy picker

Lists every window across every session as `session:index  window_name`. Selecting one switches to that session then that window.

**File to create:** `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish`

```fish
function _twf_function --description "Fuzzy-pick any window across all sessions"
  set -l selected (tmux list-windows -a -F '#{session_name}:#{window_index}  #{window_name}' 2>/dev/null \
    | fzf --prompt="window> " --height=40% \
          --preview='set t (string split "  " {})[1]; tmux list-panes -t $t -F "#P: #{pane_current_command}  #{pane_current_path}" 2>/dev/null')

  if test -z "$selected"
    return
  end

  set -l target (string split '  ' $selected)[1]   # "session:index"
  set -l parts (string split ':' $target)
  set -l sess $parts[1]
  set -l widx $parts[2]

  if test -n "$TMUX"
    tmux switch-client -t "$sess"
    tmux select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  else
    tmux attach-session -t "$sess" \; select-window -t "$sess:$widx"
  end
end
```

Register in **`home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix`**:
- Add `"_twf_function"` to the `xdg.configFile` function list (alphabetical between `_tss_function` and `_two_function`)
- Add `twf = "_twf_function";` to `shellAbbrs`

---

### 3. `tmux.conf` — last-session toggle + window picker bind

Add two lines to **`home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf`** in the bindings section:

```
# Last session (quick flip)
bind Tab switch-client -l

# Cross-session window picker
bind W run-shell "tmux new-window 'fish -c _twf_function'"
```

`Tab` is currently unbound. `W` is currently unbound (lowercase `w` is default select-window by index, not bound here).

---

## Files to Modify/Create

| File | Action |
|------|--------|
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_tss_function.fish` | Add preview + ctrl-x kill to fzf call |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/functions/_twf_function.fish` | **create** — cross-session window picker |
| `home-manager/programs/fish/default.nix` | Add `_twf_function` to list + `twf` abbr |
| `home-manager/programs/tmux/tmux.conf` | Add `bind Tab switch-client -l` and `bind W` |

## Verification

1. `tss` → hover over a session → right panel shows numbered windows
2. `tss` → hover over a session → press `ctrl-x` → session is killed, picker closes
3. `twf` (or `prefix+W` inside tmux) → lists all windows from all sessions → select one → lands in the right session+window
4. Inside tmux, press `prefix+Tab` → switches to the previously active session instantly
